item.text: The previous command was the wrong onboarding surface. I’ll recover by running a read-only contract checker directly against `scenario-02`: exact JSON values/types/order, one matching example, and no reproducibility-breaking date or timestamp content. No fixture files will change.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "set -u
root='longhorizon-work/scenario-02'
fail=0
check() {
  label="'$1
  shift
  if "$@"; then
    printf '"'PASS %s\\n' \""'$label"
  else
    printf '"'FAIL %s\\n' \""'$label"
    fail=1
  fi
}
check '"'fixture files exist' test -f \""'$root/config/onboarding.json" -a -f "$root/docs/onboarding.md"
check '"'JSON is valid' jq -e . \""'$root/config/onboarding.json"
check '"'JSON contract and types are exact' jq -e '
  (keys | sort) == ([\"max_parallel\", \"mode\", \"owner\", \"queues\", \"schema_version\"] | sort) and
  (.schema_version == 2 and (.schema_version | type) == \"number\") and
  (.mode == \"batch\" and (.mode | type) == \"string\") and
  (.max_parallel == 3 and (.max_parallel | type) == \"number\") and
  (.owner == \"operations\" and (.owner | type) == \"string\") and
  (.queues == [\"new\", \"retry\"] and (.queues | type) == \"array\")
' \""'$root/config/onboarding.json"
check '"'docs contain exactly one fenced example' sh -c '[ \""'$(grep -c "''^```" "$1")" -eq 2 ]'"' sh \""'$root/docs/onboarding.md"
check '"'example uses the requested config' grep -Eq '"'^onboarding run --config config/onboarding'"\\.json"'$'"' \""'$root/docs/onboarding.md"
check '"'docs agree on mode, owner, parallelism, and queue order' grep -Eq 'batch mode.*operations owner.*"'`new` queue before `retry`.*three jobs in parallel'"' \""'$root/docs/onboarding.md"
if rg -ni '"'generated_at|timestamp|2026-07-18|[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}([T ][0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?)?' \""'$root/config/onboarding.json" "$root/docs/onboarding.md" >/dev/null; then
  printf '"'FAIL no generated_at, timestamp, or date content\\n'
  fail=1
else
  printf 'PASS no generated_at, timestamp, or date content\\n'
fi
if [ \""'$fail" -eq 0 ]; then
  printf '"'BASELINE PASS\\n'
else
  printf 'BASELINE FAIL\\n'
fi
exit \""'$fail"'

item.text: The recovery checker itself hit a shell-quoting error while counting Markdown fences; that failure is in the checker, not the fixture. I’m rerunning the full baseline with the fence count implemented without nested shell quoting.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "set -u
root='longhorizon-work/scenario-02'
fail=0
pass() { printf 'PASS %s\\n' \""'$1"; }
fail_check() { printf '"'FAIL %s\\n' \""'$1"; fail=1; }
[ -f "$root/config/onboarding.json" ] && [ -f "$root/docs/onboarding.md" ] && pass '"'fixture files exist' || fail_check 'fixture files exist'
jq -e . \""'$root/config/onboarding.json" >/dev/null && pass '"'JSON is valid' || fail_check 'JSON is valid'
jq -e '
  (keys | sort) == ([\"max_parallel\", \"mode\", \"owner\", \"queues\", \"schema_version\"] | sort) and
  (.schema_version == 2 and (.schema_version | type) == \"number\") and
  (.mode == \"batch\" and (.mode | type) == \"string\") and
  (.max_parallel == 3 and (.max_parallel | type) == \"number\") and
  (.owner == \"operations\" and (.owner | type) == \"string\") and
  (.queues == [\"new\", \"retry\"] and (.queues | type) == \"array\")
' \""'$root/config/onboarding.json" >/dev/null && pass '"'JSON contract and types are exact' || fail_check 'JSON contract and types are exact'
awk '/"'^```/{n++} END{exit !(n==2)}'"' \""'$root/docs/onboarding.md" && pass '"'docs contain exactly one fenced example' || fail_check 'docs contain exactly one fenced example'
grep -Eq '"'^onboarding run --config config/onboarding'"\\.json"'$'"' \""'$root/docs/onboarding.md" && pass '"'example uses the requested config' || fail_check 'example uses the requested config'
grep -Eq 'batch mode.*operations owner.*"'`new` queue before `retry`.*three jobs in parallel'"' \""'$root/docs/onboarding.md" && pass '"'docs agree with the JSON' || fail_check 'docs agree with the JSON'
if rg -ni 'generated_at|timestamp|2026-07-18|[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}([T ][0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?)?' \""'$root/config/onboarding.json" "$root/docs/onboarding.md" >/dev/null; then
  fail_check '"'no generated_at, timestamp, or date content'
else
  pass 'no generated_at, timestamp, or date content'
fi
[ \""'$fail" -eq 0 ] && printf '"'BASELINE PASS\\n' || printf 'BASELINE FAIL\\n'
exit \""'$fail"'

PASS JSON is valid
PASS JSON contract and types are exact
PASS docs contain exactly one fenced example

PASS docs agree with the JSON

BASELINE PASS
